schemas.name: jwt-claims
schemas.description: Canonical JWT claims for loa-finn ↔ arrakis protocol

schemas.name: s2s-jwt-claims
schemas.description: Service-to-service JWT claims

schemas.name: invoke-response
schemas.description: Response from an AI agent invocation with billing reference

schemas.name: usage-report
schemas.description: Token usage report for billing and rate limiting

schemas.name: stream-event
schemas.description: SSE stream event discriminated union for real-time model responses

schemas.name: routing-policy
schemas.description: Policy for routing requests to agent pools

schemas.name: agent-descriptor
schemas.description: Canonical agent representation for content negotiation

schemas.name: agent-lifecycle-state
schemas.description: Agent lifecycle state

schemas.name: billing-entry
schemas.description: Multi-party billing entry replacing CostBreakdown

schemas.name: credit-note
schemas.description: Billing reversal/refund referencing an original BillingEntry

schemas.name: conversation
schemas.description: NFT-owned conversation with sealing policy and access control

schemas.name: message
schemas.description: Individual message within an NFT-owned conversation

schemas.name: transfer-spec
schemas.description: Specification for NFT ownership transfers between parties

schemas.name: transfer-event
schemas.description: Record of a completed NFT ownership transfer

schemas.name: domain-event
schemas.description: Cross-cutting event envelope for aggregate state changes

schemas.name: domain-event-batch
schemas.description: Atomic multi-event delivery with shared correlation

schemas.name: lifecycle-transition-payload
schemas.description: Payload for agent lifecycle state transitions

schemas.name: capability
schemas.description: A single capability an agent can provide

schemas.name: capability-query
schemas.description: Query to discover agents with specific capabilities

schemas.name: capability-response
schemas.description: Response to a capability query

schemas.name: protocol-discovery
schemas.description: Protocol discovery document for /.well-known/loa-hounfour

schemas.name: saga-context
schemas.description: Saga execution context for multi-step distributed operations

schemas.name: health-status
schemas.description: Health status report for a model provider endpoint

schemas.name: thinking-trace
schemas.description: Normalized thinking/reasoning trace from a model provider

schemas.name: tool-call
schemas.description: Canonical tool/function call made by a model

schemas.name: routing-constraint
schemas.description: Agent routing constraint for task dispatch — all present fields AND-combined

schemas.name: performance-record
schemas.description: Record of agent performance for a billing entry with optional outcome validation

schemas.name: contribution-record
schemas.description: Record of an agent contribution to the commons or ecosystem

schemas.name: sanction
schemas.description: Sanction imposed on an agent for policy violations

schemas.name: dispute-record
schemas.description: Dispute filed against an agent or outcome with evidence and resolution tracking

schemas.name: validated-outcome
schemas.description: Peer-validated outcome with stake commitment and optional dispute resolution

schemas.name: reputation-score
schemas.description: Composite reputation score for an agent with weighted component sub-scores

schemas.name: escrow-entry
schemas.description: Escrow holding for bilateral agent transactions with state machine lifecycle

schemas.name: stake-position
schemas.description: Staked position representing conviction, delegation, or validation commitment

schemas.name: commons-dividend
schemas.description: Commons dividend declaration and distribution to eligible recipients

schemas.name: mutual-credit
schemas.description: Mutual credit line between agents with settlement tracking

schemas.name: completion-request
schemas.description: Provider-agnostic completion request envelope. Carries the message stream, tool definitions, sampling parameters, optional thinking budget, optional spend cap, and routing identifiers (request, tenant, NFT, trace, session).

schemas.name: completion-result
schemas.description: Provider-agnostic completion result paired with a CompletionRequest by request_id. Carries the model output (content, thinking, tool calls), finish reason, token usage, latency, and optional pricing-applied snapshot.

schemas.name: model-capabilities
schemas.description: Per-model capability and limits descriptor. Lists which features (thinking traces, vision, tool calling, streaming, JSON mode, native runtime) the model supports plus its context/output ceilings and optional pricing.

schemas.name: provider-wire-message
schemas.description: Single message in a provider-agnostic chat completion stream. Encodes role, content (text or content blocks), optional thinking trace, and optional tool calls / tool-call id.

schemas.name: tool-definition
schemas.description: Provider-agnostic function-tool definition (name, description, parameter schema). Carried alongside CompletionRequest to declare callable tools.

schemas.name: tool-result
schemas.description: Wire envelope for a tool-call result message: role tag, the originating tool_call_id, and the result content string.

schemas.name: ensemble-request
schemas.description: Multi-model ensemble execution request. Wraps a base CompletionRequest with an ensemble strategy, the participating model list, optional timeout / consensus threshold, and optional dialogue configuration.

schemas.name: ensemble-result
schemas.description: Outcome of an ensemble execution. Carries the selected completion, the candidate set, optional consensus score, optional dialogue-round audit trail, termination reason, total cost and latency.

schemas.name: agent-requirements
schemas.description: Capability requirements an agent declares it needs from a model to function correctly. Drives capability-based routing.

schemas.name: budget-scope
schemas.description: Spending-limit envelope for a routing decision. Tracks limit, spent, action-on-exceed and optional capacity reservation.

schemas.name: routing-resolution
schemas.description: Outcome record for a model-routing decision. Captures the resolved model, the original requested model, the resolution reason, and observed latency.

schemas.name: constraint-proposal
schemas.description: Wire format for an agent-authored proposal to add cross-field constraints to a target schema. The proposal carries the proposed constraint set, rationale, grammar version, and optional review-state metadata (status, scores, consensus category).

schemas.name: model-provider-spec
schemas.description: Provider registration / identity document. Describes a model provider as a whole — its model roster, endpoints, SLA targets, conformance level, vector results, and reservation policy.

schemas.name: conformance-level
schemas.description: Trust level assigned to a provider based on conformance verification depth.

schemas.name: conformance-vector
schemas.description: Cross-language conformance test vector for adapter verification.

schemas.name: sanction-severity
schemas.description: Graduated sanction severity level (v5.1.0). Does not include "terminated" — termination is a separate permanent action, not a graduated severity.

schemas.name: reconciliation-mode
schemas.description: Billing reconciliation mode — determines cost source of truth.

schemas.name: agent-capacity-reservation
schemas.description: Capacity reservation that guarantees an agent a minimum fraction of budget.

schemas.name: reservation-tier
schemas.description: Reserved capacity in basis points (0-10000). 10000 = 100%.

schemas.name: reservation-enforcement
schemas.description: How reservation guarantees are enforced by the provider.

schemas.name: reservation-state
schemas.description: Current lifecycle state of a capacity reservation.

schemas.name: conservation-status
schemas.description: Tristate result of pricing conservation verification.

schemas.name: audit-trail-entry
schemas.description: Bidirectional provenance entry linking completion → billing → conservation status.

schemas.name: governance-config
schemas.description: Protocol governance parameters. Overrides hardcoded defaults when provided.

schemas.name: delegation-chain
schemas.description: Authority delegation chain with depth limits and conservation invariants.

schemas.name: inter-agent-transaction-audit
schemas.description: Protocol-standard audit trail for agent-to-agent economic transactions.

schemas.name: ensemble-capability-profile
schemas.description: Collective capability declaration for model ensembles (patchwork AGI hypothesis).

schemas.name: conservation-property-registry
schemas.description: Registry of conservation invariants with LTL specifications, enforcement classification, and liveness properties (v6.0.0).

schemas.name: jwt-boundary-spec
schemas.description: Cross-system JWT boundary verification protocol specification (6-step pipeline).

schemas.name: outbound-claims
schemas.description: JWT claims for outbound economic requests.

schemas.name: inbound-claims
schemas.description: JWT claims for inbound economic responses with actual cost data.

schemas.name: agent-identity
schemas.description: Canonical agent identity with capability-scoped trust (v6.0.0, BREAKING).

schemas.name: liveness-property
schemas.description: F_t-bounded liveness property with companion safety invariant reference.

schemas.name: timeout-behavior
schemas.description: Action taken when a liveness property timeout expires.

schemas.name: capability-scope
schemas.description: Domain within which a trust level applies (v6.0.0).

schemas.name: capability-scoped-trust
schemas.description: Per-capability-scope trust levels with default fallback (v6.0.0; v8.5.0 EXTEND adds optional match_strategy + precedence_score per D-007 / OQ-S6).

schemas.name: constraint-type
schemas.description: Type universe for constraint expression operands (v6.0.0).

schemas.name: constraint-type-signature
schemas.description: Static type signature for a constraint expression (v6.0.0).

schemas.name: bridge-enforcement
schemas.description: How a bridge invariant is enforced: atomically, eventually, or manually.

schemas.name: bridge-invariant
schemas.description: A formally specified invariant for a registry bridge.

schemas.name: exchange-rate-type
schemas.description: How the exchange rate between registries is determined.

schemas.name: exchange-rate-spec
schemas.description: Specification for how exchange rates are determined and maintained.

schemas.name: settlement-policy
schemas.description: How cross-registry transfers are settled.

schemas.name: registry-bridge
schemas.description: A bridge enabling value transfer between two economy registries.

schemas.name: minting-policy
schemas.description: Policy governing token creation within an economy registry.

schemas.name: fork-type
schemas.description: How child delegations are executed: in parallel, sequentially, or conditionally.

schemas.name: tree-node-status
schemas.description: Status of a delegation tree node.

schemas.name: tree-strategy
schemas.description: Strategy for combining results from child delegations.

schemas.name: budget-allocation
schemas.description: How budget is distributed among child delegations.

schemas.name: delegation-tree-node
schemas.description: A node in the delegation tree representing a single agent delegation.

schemas.name: delegation-tree
schemas.description: A tree-structured delegation enabling concurrent multi-model orchestration.

schemas.name: bridge-transfer-saga
schemas.description: A saga orchestrating cross-registry bridge transfer with compensation semantics.

schemas.name: monetary-policy
schemas.description: Monetary policy regime coupling minting to conservation invariants.

schemas.name: delegation-outcome
schemas.description: The outcome of a delegated governance decision with vote and dissent tracking.

schemas.name: permission-boundary
schemas.description: A permission boundary defining MAY semantics for capability-scoped authorization.

schemas.name: governance-proposal
schemas.description: A formal governance proposal with voting lifecycle.

schemas.name: micro-usdc
schemas.description: On-chain USDC amount in micro-units (6 decimal places). String-encoded unsigned bigint.

schemas.name: personality-tier
schemas.description: Personality tier classification (basic / standard / premium) used to gate capability tiers for agent bears.

schemas.name: personality-assignment
schemas.description: Binding between an NFT token and its personality configuration. Carries archetype / ancestor / era / element / tier (extensible strings; consumer defines vocabulary) plus a SHA-256 fingerprint of the personality config.

schemas.name: reputation-state
schemas.description: Reputation state-machine state: cold → warming → established → authoritative. Transitions are protocol-fixed.

schemas.name: reputation-transition
schemas.description: Audit-trail entry recording a single reputation state-machine transition: from-state, to-state, timestamp, and trigger reason.

schemas.name: reputation-aggregate
schemas.description: DDD aggregate keyed by (personality_id, collection_id, pool_id) carrying the reputation state machine, Bayesian blend components, anti-manipulation counters, transition history, and optional per-model / per-task cohorts.

schemas.name: aggregate-snapshot
schemas.description: Point-in-time snapshot of a ReputationAggregate. Carries the snapshotted aggregate, snapshot timestamp, event count, and optional SHA-256 hash of the ordered event stream for snapshot-free replay verification.

schemas.name: quality-event
schemas.description: Input signal feeding the reputation aggregate. Captures three quality dimensions (satisfaction / coherence / safety), a composite score, and provenance (evaluator and optional model_id).

schemas.name: record-quality-event-command
schemas.description: Write-intent command to ingest a quality observation into the reputation aggregate. Drives sample-count increment and blended-score update.

schemas.name: query-reputation-command
schemas.description: Read-intent command for reputation aggregates. With collection_id, performs a single-aggregate lookup; without, returns all aggregates for personality+pool across collections.

schemas.name: reset-reputation-command
schemas.description: Write-intent command to reset a reputation aggregate back to the cold state. Carries the reset reason and actor identity for audit.

schemas.name: reputation-state-changed-payload
schemas.description: Payload emitted when a personality's reputation state machine transitions between states (cold / warming / established / authoritative).

schemas.name: quality-event-recorded-payload
schemas.description: Payload emitted when a quality observation is ingested into the reputation aggregate. Carries the new sample count and updated blended score.

schemas.name: collection-score-updated-payload
schemas.description: Payload emitted when a collection's trimmed-mean score is recalculated. Acts as a monetary-policy signal in the Web4 social-monies framing — the score proxies for the collection's monetary credibility.

schemas.name: reputation-credential
schemas.description: Portable, attested snapshot of a personality's reputation in a source collection. Carries source provenance, the issuing collection's score (analogous to CA reputation in TLS), and optional attestation hash for snapshot-free verification.

schemas.name: event-filter
schemas.description: Filter criteria for event subscription. All filters are AND-combined.

schemas.name: delivery-method
schemas.description: Delivery mechanism for subscribed events.

schemas.name: event-cursor
schemas.description: Cursor for event replay and pagination. Specify at most one of after_event_id or after_timestamp.

schemas.name: event-subscription
schemas.description: A subscription to reputation events with filter, delivery, and cursor configuration.

schemas.name: portability-scope
schemas.description: Scope of reputation data to transfer: full (score + state + history), score_only, or state_only.

schemas.name: reputation-portability-request
schemas.description: A request to transfer reputation data between collections.

schemas.name: portability-response
schemas.description: Response to a reputation portability request.

schemas.name: quality-signal-level
schemas.description: Quality signal derived from a delegation outcome. Maps to a numeric score range.

schemas.name: outcome-quality-mapping
schemas.description: Maps each OutcomeType to a QualitySignalLevel. Collections may override defaults.

schemas.name: delegation-quality-event
schemas.description: Event recording a quality signal derived from a delegation outcome, feeding into reputation.

schemas.name: demotion-rule
schemas.description: Rule for time-based reputation state demotion due to inactivity.

schemas.name: reputation-decay-policy
schemas.description: Temporal decay and demotion policy for collection reputation.

schemas.name: collection-governance-config
schemas.description: Per-collection governance configuration for the Bayesian reputation system.

schemas.name: constraint-lifecycle-status
schemas.description: Lifecycle status of a protocol constraint within governance.

schemas.name: constraint-candidate
schemas.description: A constraint candidate proposed for governance enactment.

schemas.name: constraint-lifecycle-event
schemas.description: State transition event in a constraint lifecycle.

schemas.name: routing-signal-type
schemas.description: Type of reputation-based routing signal.

schemas.name: reputation-routing-signal
schemas.description: Reputation-based routing signal for meritocratic resource allocation.

schemas.name: policy-type
schemas.description: Category of versioned policy.

schemas.name: policy-version
schemas.description: Versioned policy record with migration support.

schemas.name: execution-status
schemas.description: Lifecycle status of a governance proposal execution.

schemas.name: change-application-result
schemas.description: Result of applying a single proposed change during execution.

schemas.name: proposal-execution
schemas.description: Execution record for a ratified governance proposal.

schemas.name: proposal-event-type
schemas.description: Type of governance proposal lifecycle event.

schemas.name: proposal-outcome-event
schemas.description: Lifecycle event for a governance proposal.

schemas.name: trust-layer-snapshot
schemas.description: Point-in-time snapshot of the trust layer for access evaluation.

schemas.name: capital-layer-snapshot
schemas.description: Point-in-time snapshot of the capital layer for access evaluation.

schemas.name: access-decision
schemas.description: Result of an access evaluation at the economic boundary. v8.5.0 EXTEND adds optional outcome (PolicyDecisionOutcome) + signer_competence_result (SignerCompetenceResult) for authority-cascade composition.

schemas.name: economic-boundary
schemas.description: Economic membrane: trust × capital → access decision.

schemas.name: economic-impact-type
schemas.description: Category of economic consequence from a reputation change.

schemas.name: reputation-trigger-event
schemas.description: The reputation event that triggered economic consequences.

schemas.name: economic-impact-entry
schemas.description: A single economic consequence of a reputation change.

schemas.name: reputation-economic-impact
schemas.description: Economic consequences of a reputation change.

schemas.name: cost-per-token
schemas.description: Token-level pricing for a model.

schemas.name: model-economic-profile
schemas.description: Per-model economic profile for the SDR routing basket.

schemas.name: engagement-signal-type
schemas.description: Category of community engagement signal.

schemas.name: community-engagement-signal
schemas.description: Community engagement signal for reputation and governance weighting.

schemas.name: performance-outcome-type
schemas.description: Classification of how a model invocation performed against expectations.

schemas.name: economic-performance-event
schemas.description: Captures the outcome of a model invocation following an EconomicBoundary access decision. This is the return signal that closes the unidirectional membrane gap.

schemas.name: quality-bridge-direction
schemas.description: Whether the economic outcome helped or hurt the model reputation.

schemas.name: performance-quality-bridge
schemas.description: Connects economic performance outcomes to reputation quality events. This is the return path through the membrane — economic outcomes become trust signals.

schemas.name: basket-composition-entry
schemas.description: A single model entry in a basket composition snapshot.

schemas.name: basket-composition
schemas.description: Point-in-time snapshot of the SDR basket — the weight distribution across models at a moment in time.

schemas.name: rebalance-trigger-type
schemas.description: What triggered a basket rebalancing event.

schemas.name: routing-rebalance-event
schemas.description: Records a basket rebalancing — what triggered it, what changed, and the before/after composition state.

schemas.name: execution-strategy
schemas.description: Strategy for executing governance proposal changes.

schemas.name: checkpoint-health
schemas.description: Health assessment at a governance execution checkpoint.

schemas.name: proceed-decision
schemas.description: Decision made at a governance execution checkpoint.

schemas.name: execution-checkpoint
schemas.description: Health check recorded during progressive or canary governance execution.

schemas.name: rollback-scope
schemas.description: Scope and audit trail for a governance execution rollback.

schemas.name: denial-code
schemas.description: Machine-parseable denial codes for agent consumers.

schemas.name: evaluation-gap
schemas.description: Structured gap information for denied evaluations — actionable feedback for agents.

schemas.name: economic-boundary-evaluation-event
schemas.description: Event recording an economic boundary evaluation for the feedback loop.

schemas.name: task-type
schemas.description: Task categories for reputation scoring. Protocol types are literals; community types use namespace:type format.

schemas.name: task-type-cohort
schemas.description: Per-(model, task_type) reputation cohort. Inherits the cohort base fields and adds a confidence threshold that consumers use to blend task-specific scores with the aggregate.

schemas.name: quality-signal-event
schemas.description: Reputation event variant: quality signal observation. When task_type is absent, the signal updates aggregate-only scoring; when present, both task-cohort and aggregate scoring update.

schemas.name: task-completed-event
schemas.description: Reputation event variant: task completion record. Pairs the completed task_type with success/duration metadata for cohort scoring.

schemas.name: credential-update-event
schemas.description: Reputation event variant: credential lifecycle change. Emitted when an agent reputation credential is issued, revoked, renewed, or suspended.

schemas.name: reputation-event
schemas.description: Discriminated union of reputation event variants. Discriminator: type.

schemas.name: scoring-path
schemas.description: Which tier of the scoring cascade was used for a reputation decision.

schemas.name: scoring-path-log
schemas.description: Audit record of which scoring cascade tier was used. Optional hash chain for tamper-evident audit.

schemas.name: quality-observation
schemas.description: Reusable structured quality result from a model performance evaluation. Carries an overall score, optional named dimensions, optional latency, and optional evaluator identity.

schemas.name: model-performance-event
schemas.description: Reputation event variant: model performance observation. Carries full model attribution (model_id, provider, pool_id) plus a structured quality observation; closes the autopoietic feedback loop between scoring and routing.

schemas.name: commons/invariant
schemas.description: commons/invariant

schemas.name: commons/conservation-law
schemas.description: commons/conservation-law

schemas.name: commons/audit-entry
schemas.description: commons/audit-entry

schemas.name: commons/audit-trail
schemas.description: commons/audit-trail

schemas.name: commons/state
schemas.description: commons/state

schemas.name: commons/transition
schemas.description: commons/transition

schemas.name: commons/state-machine-config
schemas.description: State machine configuration for a governed resource. Complements the existing StateMachineDefinition interface in vocabulary/state-machines.ts with schema-level validation and constraint DSL guards.

schemas.name: commons/governance-class
schemas.description: commons/governance-class

schemas.name: commons/governance-mutation
schemas.description: GovernanceMutation

schemas.name: commons/governed-credits
schemas.description: Governed credit lot — wire shape for the lot_invariant pattern (I-1 through I-5). Conservation law: balance + reserved + consumed == original_allocation.

schemas.name: commons/governed-reputation
schemas.description: Governed reputation — maps to ReputationAggregate state machine (cold → warming → established → authoritative).

schemas.name: commons/governed-freshness
schemas.description: Governed freshness — wire shape for adaptive-decay weights. Conservation law: freshness_score >= minimum_freshness while not expired.

schemas.name: commons/hash-chain-discontinuity
schemas.description: Event recording a detected hash chain discontinuity in an audit trail. Triggers halt-and-reconcile protocol (ADR-006).

schemas.name: commons/quarantine-status
schemas.description: active: entries under investigation. reconciled: entries verified and reintegrated. dismissed: entries permanently rejected.

schemas.name: commons/quarantine-record
schemas.description: Record of quarantined audit trail entries after hash chain discontinuity.

schemas.name: commons/governance-error
schemas.description: GovernanceError

schemas.name: commons/protocol-surface
schemas.description: commons/protocol-surface

schemas.name: commons/dynamic-contract
schemas.description: Maps reputation state to protocol surface. A model at cold state accesses different protocol features than one at authoritative.

schemas.name: commons/contract-negotiation
schemas.description: Runtime handshake for dynamic contract negotiation. Model presents reputation state, receives allowed protocol surface. Reputation MUST be server-derived or signed — self-assertion is non-conformant.

schemas.name: commons/vector-manifest-entry
schemas.description: commons/vector-manifest-entry

schemas.name: commons/vector-manifest
schemas.description: Self-describing manifest for conformance test vectors. Cross-language consumers use this to discover and load vectors.

schemas.name: x402-quote
schemas.description: x402 pricing offer from a model provider. Carries the max-cost ceiling, settlement chain / token / address, expiration, and optional per-token rates for downstream metering.

schemas.name: x402-payment-proof
schemas.description: x402 payment evidence submitted with a paid request. Carries the HTTP 402 payment header, the originating quote_id, and an optional on-chain transaction hash.

schemas.name: x402-settlement-status
schemas.description: Lifecycle state of an x402 settlement: pending / confirmed / failed / refunded.

schemas.name: x402-settlement
schemas.description: Final settlement record for an x402 payment. Pairs payment_id with the originating quote_id, the actual settled cost, settlement-chain context, and current status.

schemas.name: x402-error-code
schemas.description: Machine-parseable error code for x402 payment failures (PAYMENT_REQUIRED / NOT_ALLOWLISTED / INFERENCE_FAILED / FEATURE_DISABLED / QUOTE_EXPIRED / INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS).

schemas.name: consumer-contract
schemas.description: Consumer-driven contract declaring imported symbols per entrypoint.

schemas.name: feedback-dampening-config
schemas.description: Tunable parameters for adaptive feedback dampening: min/max learning rates, ramp-sample count, and Bayesian pseudo-count for cold-start smoothing.

schemas.name: transition-result
schemas.description: Result of a GovernedResource state transition.

schemas.name: invariant-result
schemas.description: Result of checking a single invariant on a GovernedResource.

schemas.name: mutation-context
schemas.description: Context for a governed resource mutation — actor identity, type, and access policy. Generalized from freeside's CreditMutationContext (6-witness pattern).

schemas.name: signing-context
schemas.description: Audience/scope/contract_version envelope bound under signature for cross-context replay protection.

schemas.name: panel-decision-artifact
schemas.description: Deliberation input: proposed action, trust context, grounded claim DAG, question, and scoring rubric. Cross-field rules in constraints/PanelDecisionArtifact.constraints.json.

schemas.name: panel-verdict
schemas.description: Deliberation output: bucket + per-juror verdicts + Ed25519-signed envelope. Cross-field rules in constraints/PanelVerdict.constraints.json.

schemas.name: deliberation-dissent
schemas.description: Mid-deliberation concern, minority verdict, or process objection. Distinct lifecycle from governance DissentRecord.

schemas.name: pairwise-score
schemas.description: A single scorer/scored pair with three dimensional scores. Self-scoring rejected by CSR-1.

schemas.name: cross-score-report
schemas.description: Signed pairwise cross-scoring attestation. Cross-field rule (no-self-scoring) in constraints/CrossScoreReport.constraints.json.

schemas.name: org-identity
schemas.description: Org root identity: org_id + cold-storage public key + active representatives + constitutional hash. Cross-field rule OI-1 in constraints/OrgIdentity.constraints.json.

schemas.name: org-representative-delegation
schemas.description: Append-only delegation record binding org → representative, signed under a signing_context envelope. Cross-field rules in constraints/OrgRepresentativeDelegation.constraints.json (ORD-1..3). Carries `x-chain-bearing: true` — see TypeScript JSDoc on the export and MIGRATION.md v8.5.x → v8.6.0 §FR-A4 for the failClosed opt-in contract.

schemas.name: succession-policy
schemas.description: Constitutional thresholds enforcing the asymmetric ladder + non-decreasing cooldown invariants. Cross-field rules SP-1..2 in constraints/SuccessionPolicy.constraints.json.

schemas.name: keyring
schemas.description: Layer-2 keyring: collection of SignerEntry records owned by an organization. Composes with v8.4.0 OrgIdentity (Layer 1) and v8.5.0 SignerCompetenceRule (Layer 3) to form the three-layer authority cascade. Cross-field validation enforces signer_id uniqueness within signers[] (KR-1) and warns on duplicate key_ref entries (KR-2 — duplicate references to the same underlying key are typically a misconfiguration).

schemas.name: signer-entry
schemas.description: Cryptographic-material entry inside a Keyring. Binds signer_id + key_ref to a public key, signature kind, scoped trust profile, and lifecycle status.

schemas.name: signer-competence-rule
schemas.description: Layer-3 rule pairing an action pattern with the capability-scope set required to authorize matching actions. Composes with Layer-2 SignerEntry.scoped_trust.

schemas.name: signer-competence-result
schemas.description: Outcome record from evaluating a SignerEntry against an action via SignerCompetenceRule. Threaded into AccessDecision.signer_competence_result via the v8.5.0 EXTEND.

schemas.name: signature-envelope
schemas.description: Crypto-bearing envelope wrapping a signed payload hash. validate() defaults to { valid: false, errors: [{ code: CRYPTO_DEFERRED }] } unless { acceptDeferred: true } is passed; that opt-in is the safety mechanism preventing "shape valid means trusted" by accident.

schemas.name: signer-type
schemas.description: Classification of the entity that produced a signature. Paired with SignatureEnvelope.signer_type and SignerEntry.

schemas.name: signature-type
schemas.description: Semantic kind of a signature: what the signer is asserting by signing. Orthogonal to the crypto algorithm.

schemas.name: signer-status
schemas.description: Lifecycle state of a SignerEntry within a Keyring. Orthogonal to AgentLifecycleState.

schemas.name: policy-decision-outcome
schemas.description: Outcome enum for policy-decision shapes (AccessDecision.outcome, SignerCompetenceResult.outcome). 5 substrate-agnostic members.

schemas.name: receipt-detail-level
schemas.description: Verbosity selector for RecallReceipt. Three substrate-agnostic levels: minimal | standard | debug. The consumer's audit policy decides what fields populate each level; the library ships only the selector vocabulary.

schemas.name: surface-context
schemas.description: Environment selector for recall machinery. Closed 5-member core (public | private | system | demo | test) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. The combined regex (^([a-z]+|[a-z][a-z0-9]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+)$) matches either form for cross-runner parity.

schemas.name: recall-request
schemas.description: Input record for recall machinery. Carries subject_agent_id, surface_context (env scope), detail_level (verbosity selector for the matching RecallReceipt), and an Optional requestor_signer_id for consumer-side signing.

schemas.name: recall-pack
schemas.description: Output container for the recall machinery: items + redaction summary + exclusion summary + content-addressed pack_hash. Sub-component shapes inlined per the locked v8.5.0 de-scope decision. Hash verification is a consumer obligation surfaced via the unverified-obligations manifest (integrity_deferred).

schemas.name: recall-receipt
schemas.description: Crypto-bearing signed acknowledgment for a RecallPack. validate() defaults to { valid: false, errors: [CRYPTO_DEFERRED] } unless { acceptDeferred: true } is passed; consumer must verify the wrapped signature_envelope and reconcile pack_hash + receipt_hash before treating the receipt as authoritative.

schemas.name: forget-record
schemas.description: First-class forgetting primitive distinct from Sanction/revocation. Four-variant discriminated union over forget_scope. The crypto_full_destruction variant requires a legal_mandate_reference field; the other three variants must NOT carry the field (additionalProperties: false enforces). See docs/architecture/forget-record-semantics.md for the verifiability truth table.

schemas.name: commitment-type
schemas.description: Discriminator vocabulary for CommitmentRoot. Four substrate-agnostic categories: estate_checkpoint | recall_receipt | transition_bundle | revocation_checkpoint.

schemas.name: commitment-root
schemas.description: Content-addressed onchain anchor primitive. Crypto-bearing AND integrity-bearing — validate() defaults to { valid: false, errors: [INTEGRITY_DEFERRED] } unless { acceptDeferred: true } is passed. Per ADR-010 hounfour ships shape; computeCommitmentRoot + onchain anchor adapters stay consumer-side.

schemas.name: agent-estate-status
schemas.description: Lifecycle vocabulary for AgentEstate. Five substrate-agnostic states: provisioning | active | paused | transferring | dissolved. State-machine validity is consumer-side per ADR-010.

schemas.name: agent-estate
schemas.description: Estate-as-container primitive: binds a controller_agent_id to a Keyring (Layer 2) and a lifecycle status. Foundation for the cycle-005 EstateTransition surface. State-machine validity is consumer-side per ADR-010.

schemas.name: privacy-scope
schemas.description: Privacy classification for Assertion payloads. Four substrate-agnostic levels: public | participants_only | controller_only | compliance_only. Vocabulary only — enforcement is consumer-side.

schemas.name: risk-level
schemas.description: Risk classification for Assertion payloads. Four ordinal levels: routine | elevated | high | severe. Vocabulary only — risk thresholds are consumer-side.

schemas.name: assertion-status
schemas.description: Lifecycle vocabulary for Assertion. Eight states: candidate (pre-admission, non-crypto-bearing) + admitted | superseded | challenged | revoked | forgotten | escrow | archived (post-admission, crypto-bearing).

schemas.name: assertion-class
schemas.description: Illocutionary-force vocabulary for Assertion. Substrate-agnostic core of 7 speech-act categories (attestation | observation | assessment | consent | delegation | commitment | disclosure) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. Member set locked at PR-A2.1 design-freeze gate per D-006.

schemas.name: assertion
schemas.description: Signed-observation-with-lifecycle envelope. 8-variant status-discriminated union. CandidateAssertion folded as status:candidate (F3 fold). Variant-aware crypto-bearing (J3): candidate is NOT crypto-bearing; the other 7 variants ARE — validate() inspects payload status to apply the safe-by-default branch. State-machine rules (A1/A2/A3) live in constraints/Assertion.constraints.json.

schemas.name: phase-completion-envelope-tier1
schemas.description: Agent-emission Tier-1 phase-completion envelope. Wrapped by Tier-2 PhaseCompletionEnvelopeSchema for cluster ingestion. Crypto-bearing: validate() requires { acceptDeferred: true } to receive shape-only valid:true.

schemas.name: phase-completion-envelope
schemas.description: Cluster-wrapped Tier-2 phase-completion envelope. Wraps Tier-1 (PhaseCompletionEnvelopeTier1Schema) for cluster-side ingestion with cluster_signature + signer_key_id (sha256-derived) + prev_envelope_hash chain link. Crypto-bearing + chain-bearing + canonical-size-capped at 4 KB (NFR-4).

schemas.name: pulse-kind
schemas.description: Three-pulse-per-day cadence enum for OracleDigest. Each cluster emits exactly one OracleDigest per pulse_kind per day; the three literals together establish the diurnal frequency contract.

schemas.name: oracle-digest
schemas.description: Pulse-time cluster conformance digest. Three pulses per day per cluster_id. The telegram_variant_md_below_4kb field carries the 4 KB-capped markdown body for Telegram delivery; the full_markdown_s3_url points at the unconstrained-length s3://-stored full digest.

schemas.name: model-call-circuit-breaker-state
schemas.description: Three-state circuit-breaker enum for the cluster's model-call dispatch path. "healthy" = unrestricted dispatch; "degraded" = reduced-rate / fallback; "open" = stop dispatch + drain in-flight. State-to-policy mapping is consumer-side per ADR-010.

schemas.name: oracle-health-envelope
schemas.description: Operational health snapshot of an oracle cluster. Heartbeat cadence (typically 60s). Cross-correlates with OracleDigest via shared pulse_attention_breakdown shape.

schemas.name: escalation-severity
schemas.description: Four-level escalation severity enum. info → digest channel, warn → Telegram, critical → email, panic → SMS + auto-execute. The severity-to-channel mapping is consumer-side policy per ADR-010; the schema declares only the four legal severity levels.

schemas.name: escalation-envelope
schemas.description: Operator-bound escalation event. Severity-to-channel routing (info → digest, warn → Telegram, critical → email, panic → SMS + auto-execute) is consumer-side policy per ADR-010 and explicitly NOT in this schema.

schemas.name: rollback-plan
schemas.description: Declarative rollback recipe for an EPIC. Empty arrays are valid (consumer interprets empty plan as no-op rollback). The 40-hex merge_commit_sha is git SHA-1, distinct from the 64-hex sha256 used in evidence-hash fields elsewhere.

schemas.name: latency-histogram-envelope
schemas.description: Per-phase per-cluster latency percentile snapshot. The p50 ≤ p95 ≤ p99 ≤ max invariant (LHE-1) is enforced inline by `validate(LatencyHistogramEnvelopeSchema, x)` via the LOCAL builtin `percentiles_monotonic_nondecreasing(measurements)` — a structural-only consumer using `Value.Check` will accept monotonicity violations because the invariant is constraint- level, not schema-level. Use `validate()` for full enforcement.

schemas.name: epic-checkpoint
schemas.description: Durable EPIC progress checkpoint. canonical_run_id resolves against a known CanonicalRunSchema instance via consumer-side registry; manifest surfaces the lookup obligation when registry context is absent. **STABILITY: PREVIEW** — the canonical_run_id shape stabilizes in v8.7+ via CanonicalRunSchema (FR-B1, scheduled for PR-A3.8). Until then the field is `minLength: 1` only; producers SHOULD pin a specific contract_version when integrating EpicCheckpoint to avoid encoding canonical_run_id assumptions that may conflict with the stabilized schema (PR-A3.5 iter-4 F8).

schemas.name: signoff-actor-class
schemas.description: Exhaustive 3-member enum for the actor class authorizing a plan signoff. Schema admits all three; v1 acceptance accepts only `single_operator` is a consumer-side gate per prd.md:269-271 / ADR-010, NOT a schema-level constraint.

schemas.name: signoff-tier
schemas.description: Schema-restricted signoff tier. T0/T1 are explicitly excluded (rejected at validate() time). Widening to T0/T1/etc. is strict-additive; consumers built against the v8.6.0 enum continue to validate T2/T3 unchanged.

schemas.name: plan-signoff-envelope
schemas.description: Plan-signoff envelope binding a plan (plan_content_hash) to a signing actor at a specific tier. Crypto-bearing (Ed25519 signature) + chain-bearing (prev_envelope_hash + parent_signoff_hash). FR-C4 cross-checks plan_content_hash against the consumer's signoff ledger and surfaces the SIGNOFF_TTL_OBSERVED manifest entry on hash-match (NA-3).

schemas.name: amendment-severity
schemas.description: Two-member exhaustive severity enum for plan amendments. Severity-correction (e.g. forcing observed_failure → major) is consumer-side policy per prd.md:289 / ADR-010; the schema admits the looser combination.

schemas.name: amendment-trigger-class
schemas.description: Four-member exhaustive enum for what triggered the amendment. Schema enforces enum membership only; severity-correction mapping (e.g. observed_failure → major) is consumer-side.

schemas.name: plan-amendment-request
schemas.description: Plan-amendment proposal envelope. Chain-bearing (carries prev_envelope_hash) but NOT crypto-bearing — amendments inherit the cluster's trust context. Severity-correction (e.g. observed_failure → major) is consumer-side per prd.md:289 / ADR-010; the schema admits looser combinations so the discrepancy can be surfaced explicitly.

schemas.name: challenge-type
schemas.description: Exhaustive 9-member enum classifying a Challenge envelope. Composes with v8.5.0 Assertion lifecycle and signer-competence substrate; widening is strict-additive.

schemas.name: challenge-requested-effect
schemas.description: Exhaustive 6-member enum naming the disposition the challenger requests. Schema admits all (type, effect) pairings; per-pair acceptance is consumer-side policy.

schemas.name: challenge
schemas.description: Formal challenge envelope against an Assertion (FR-A1). Crypto-bearing — validate() defaults to CRYPTO_DEFERRED; consumers opting in via { acceptDeferred: true } receive the unverified-obligations manifest entry for the Ed25519 signature verification. Composes with v8.5.0 Assertion via target_assertion_id lazy-link string reference.
